channel.title: S21sec Cyber Solutions by Thales — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by S21sec Cyber Solutions by Thales (s21sec).

item.title: CVE-2026-22539 · 5.3 MEDIUM — INFORMATION DISCLOSURE VIA CURL REQUESTS (OCPP)
item.description: As the service interaction is performed without authentication, an attacker with some knowledge of the protocol could obtain information about the charger via OCPP v1.6.

item.title: CVE-2026-22537 · 6.8 MEDIUM — INFORMATION DISCLOSURE WITHIN THE OPERATING SYSTEM
item.description: The lack of hardening of the system allows the user used to manage and maintain the charger to consult different files containing clear-text credentials or valuable information for an attacker.

item.title: CVE-2026-22536 · 8.6 HIGH — PRIVILEGE ESCALATION VIA SUDO COMMAND
item.description: The absence of permissions control for the user XXX allows the current configuration in the sudoers file to escalate privileges without any restrictions

item.title: CVE-2026-22535 · 8.9 HIGH — FRAIL SECURITY IN MQTT PROTOCOL ALLOWS AN ATTACKER MODIFY CRITICAL PARAMETERS
item.description: An attacker with the ability to interact through the network and with access credentials, could, thanks to the unsecured (unencrypted) MQTT communications protocol, write on the server topics of the board that controls the MQTT communications

item.title: CVE-2026-22544 · 8.7 HIGH 🌩️ — EXCHANGE OF CREDENTIALS IN CLEAR TEXT
item.description: An attacker with a network connection could detect credentials in clear text.

item.title: CVE-2026-22543 · 6.9 MEDIUM — WEEK ENCODING FOR PASSWORDS
item.description: The credentials required to access the device's web server are sent in base64 within the HTTP headers. Since base64 is not considered a strong cipher, an attacker could intercept the web request handling the login and obtain the credentials

item.title: CVE-2026-22542 · 9.2 CRITICAL 🌩️ — DENIAL OF SERVICE FOR CONCURRENT CONNECTIONS ON TELNET
item.description: An attacker with access to the system's internal network can cause a denial of service on the system by making two concurrent connections through the Telnet service.

item.title: CVE-2026-22541 · 8.2 HIGH 🌩️ — DENIAL OF SERVICE VIA ICMP PACKETS
item.description: The massive sending of ICMP requests causes a denial of service on one of the boards from the EVCharger that allows control the EV interfaces. Since the board must be operating correctly for the charger to also function correctly.

item.title: CVE-2026-22540 · 9.2 CRITICAL 🌩️ — DENIAL OF SERVICE VIA ARP PACKETS
item.description: The massive sending of ARP requests causes a denial of service on one board of the charger that allows control of the EV interfaces. Since the board must be operating correctly for the charger to also function correctly.

item.title: CVE-2025-64385 · 9.2 CRITICAL 🌩️ — INCORRECT SECURITY VALIDATION IN SENDING UDP FRAMES
item.description: The equipment initially can be configured using the manufacturer's application, by Wi-Fi, by the web server or with the manufacturer’s software. Using the manufacturer's software, the device can be configured via UDP. Analyzing this communication, it…

item.title: CVE-2025-64389 · 8.3 HIGH 🌩️ — EXCHANGE OF SENSITIVE INFORMATION IN CLEAR TEXT
item.description: The web server of the device performs exchanges of sensitive information in clear text through an insecure protocol.

item.title: CVE-2025-64388 · 9.2 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Denial of service through specific packets
item.description: Denial of service of the web server through specific requests to this protocol

item.title: CVE-2025-64387 · 5.1 MEDIUM 🌩️ — CLICKJACKING
item.description: The web application is vulnerable to a so-called ‘clickjacking’ attack. In this type of attack, the vulnerable page is inserted into a page controlled by the attacker in order to deceive the victim. This deception can range from making the victim cli…

item.title: CVE-2025-64386 · 7.7 HIGH 🌩️ — HIJACKING OF THE TOKEN AND GAINING ACCESS
item.description: The equipment grants a JWT token for each connection in the timeline, but during an active valid session, a hijacking of the token can be done. This will allow an attacker with the token modify parameters of security, access or even steal the session…

item.title: CVE-2025-11155 · 6.8 MEDIUM — WEAK ENCODING FOR PASSWORD IN DEVICE SERVER CONFIGURATION
item.description: The credentials required to access the device's web server are sent in base64 within the HTTP headers. Since base64 is not considered a strong cipher, an attacker could intercept the web request handling the login and obtain the credentials.
